interactions.summary: The host asks whether countries will respond to American disorder by moving faster toward China, BRICS, and other multilateral institutions.

Jiang answers that Mark Carney's speech marks a turning point toward a genuine rules-based order centered on China, BRICS, multilateral trade, and even gold-backed discipline against American financial corruption.
The host adds that Russia's reserve strategy shows why gold is attractive and asks whether China also rejects exorbitant privilege itself.
Jiang replies that exorbitant privilege corrodes the hegemon through indiscipline, oligarchy, inequality, and complacency.

claims.claim: Jiang says Mark Carney's speech marks a turning point because it openly declares the old rules-based order a myth and the Trump-era United States a mafia empire.
Jiang says China is the main power actively advocating multilateralism through trade, infrastructure, and cooperation with developing countries.
Jiang presents the Shanghai Gold Exchange and a transparent gold ledger as part of a Chinese plan for a new order that restrains corruption and finances productive infrastructure.
Jiang says countries must move proactively into a new BRICS-shaped order now because a predatory United States will otherwise pick them off one by one.
Jiang says exorbitant privilege harms the hegemon by encouraging lack of discipline, corruption, oligarchy, inequality, and complacency.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang converts monetary reform into a moral and civilizational discipline project rather than a technical reserve-management tweak.
signature_moments.source_phrase: let's put a new order based on gold
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It shows that his China-centered order is supposed to restrain the corruption he associates with dollar hegemony.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang turns Carney's warning into a menu image in which every middle power is eventually up for coercive consumption.
signature_moments.source_phrase: eventually we're all running in a menu
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It dramatizes why passive alliance with Washington is no longer safe.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Used here for the dollar-centered power that lets the United States fund wars and corruption while exporting the cost to everyone else.
Jiang contrasts this with the old mythic order and associates it with BRICS, multilateral benefit, and protection from arbitrary empire.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang again dates this turning point to Mark Carney's recent speech, tying the packet tightly to late January 2026 rather than a timeless BRICS thesis.

uncertainty_notes.note: The description of a gold-ledger world order is presented as China's strategic vision, but the packet does not distinguish clearly between official Chinese policy and Jiang's interpretive synthesis.
